briefing.headline: Slow and natural — winter stained water is prime soft plastic territory.
briefing.gameplan:
In winter with stained water and stable conditions, soft plastics presentations shine.

Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Match the conditions — moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: '2008 Classic at Hartwell — brutal cold Day 1, hammered them cranking a weighted-to-suspend Series 5 over 15-25ft with stop-and-start retrieve. Day 2, post-frontal sunny skies killed that bite. Ran to the upper end with stained water and fish...
Yamamoto: The Senko works all year round but shines spring to fall. In colder months, fish deeper water on long underwater structure such as points with a small weight added

Wheeler: Big fish spots are big giant flats in 22-29 ft with tiny isolated rockpiles — a boulder in the middle of a rocky flat, or a hard-bottom spot. Only good for 1-2 fish and time-consuming to find, but that's what separates you

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable winter conditions position bass at 15-35ft on main lake structure — bluffs, steep points, channel bends. Fish are lethargic and grouped tight. Vertical presentations directly on structure are essential.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
